_provenance.source: Matriks MCP (interactive probe, sanctioned per CLAUDE.md §4)
_provenance.RESOLVES: The M0 foreign-flow QUIRK. The original M0 call used foreignInvestorMode='monthly' — a CURRENT-SNAPSHOT service that returns the latest month and does NOT honor foreignPeriods as a historical selector. The fix is the RANGE mode foreignInvestorMode='historic' + startDate/endDate, which works now that the MKK license is live: broker-level foreign buy/sell over the window, foreign houses classifiable (BANK-OF-AMERICA=MLB, HSBC=HSY).

_golden_master.backs: Foreign-flow factor (CLAUDE.md §5 — THE BIST comovement driver). Construction decision 2026-06-21: broker-netting (tag foreign houses, net them) + EVDS weekly non-resident holdings as the market-wide cross-check, at DAILY granularity (loop historic mode per day).
_golden_master.stable_window: A FIXED past window (2025Q1) — history does not change, so this is a value-anchored drift guard, not reachability-only.
_golden_master.OPEN_RESIDUAL: The per-investor %-non-resident demographic series (v1/investor/historic) STILL returns 400 even with the MKK license, in both the institutionalFlow and historicalData paths. Escalate to Matriks/MKK: is it a higher VAP tier, a different param shape (discrete investorDates not a range), or simply not API-exposed? The chosen broker-netting path does NOT depend on it.
_golden_master.classification_caveat: Foreign vs domestic is about non-resident CUSTODY, not the parent's nationality — e.g. GARANTI BBVA (GRM) is a DOMESTIC broker despite the BBVA (Spanish) parent. So the foreign-broker map must be a CURATED, verified reference (ideally an authoritative MKK custodian-type list), never inferred from names. Unknown brokers must be surfaced/refused, never silently bucketed as domestic.
